title: ageratina serrulata
intro: Ageratina serrulata, also known as Mexican tea or tree dandelion, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Ageratina serrulata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular remedy in folk medicine, although its effectiveness and safety should be evaluated by a healthcare professional.
